title: rubus × maceriarum
intro: Rubus × maceriarum, also known as Himalayan raspberry, is a hybrid species of the Rubus genus, resulting from the cross between Rubus idaeus and Rubus fruticosus. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, containing anthocyanins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds that exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Rubus × maceriarum include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an antiseptic and antiviral agent. Himalayan raspberry can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin problems.
